notes: Code editor for the page editor: a Visual ⇄ Code toggle in the editor toolbar opens a VS Code-style source view (CodeMirror, syntax-highlighted HTML + inline CSS/JS, dark theme, line numbers, auto-close tags, Ctrl/Cmd+S to save). Switching back to Visual ingests any hand-edits so they stay editable visually; saving in Code mode writes the source directly. Fixed a media-gallery bug where, on a filtered view (e.g. Videos), clicking a tile opened/copied the item at that slot in the unfiltered 'All' list instead of the one you clicked. Editor preview now fills the canvas: the desktop preview is the largest 16:9 box that fits (rendering at a real desktop width — at least 1440, larger on big monitors) plus a Fill-screen button (native fullscreen). Fixed self profile editing — the signed-in user's id and email are now stored in the session so the Account modal populates and saves (was 'Please sign in again'). Added custom code injection points in Analytics: Functional/Analytics/Marketing each with a header and a body field, injected into <head> and before </body> via the existing Apply-to-All-Pages sweep. Account/Settings cleanup: removed the Updates card and the Mailgun card from Settings (Mailgun is now configured server-side in config.secret.php so the API key never passes through the browser); removed the top-right notification bell; the top-right avatar is now a profile menu (Account / Change password / Sign out) and the change-password + sign-out buttons moved out of the bottom-left into it; signed-in users can now edit their own profile (name + email) via the new Account modal. ADAptify panel isolation fix: the widget is now appended to <html> (a sibling of <body>) and every visitor effect is scoped to <body>, so the panel itself can never be recolored, filtered, zoomed, or resized by the page — verified the menu stays readable under High Contrast/Invert. ADAptify full option set: the widget now offers Readable Font, Highlight Titles/Links, Text Magnifier, Content Scaling + Font Size + Line Height + Letter Spacing steppers (±10% per click), text alignment, Dark/Light/High contrast, Monochrome, Invert, High/Low saturation, custom text/title/background colors, Mute Sounds, Hide Images, Reading Guide, Reading Mask, Stop Animations, Highlight on Hover/Click, and Large Cursor — grouped into Content/Color/Navigation sections, all persisted per visitor. Updates are now ALWAYS automatic: the manual update card is removed and every site installs the latest engine at login, so no one can run a stale build (re-introduce a gate before a major release if needed). Security: enforce HTTPS for credentials. A new root .htaccess redirects all HTTP traffic to HTTPS site-wide (proxy-aware to avoid redirect loops), and api.php now refuses sign-in / password / secret actions over plain HTTP as a server-side backstop (localhost exempt; toggle with 'require_https' in config.secret.php). Passwords were already hashed at rest with Argon2id/bcrypt; this closes the in-transit gap. Hotfix (1.4.1): neutralized a literal closing-script sequence in an ADAptify comment that truncated admin/index.html and broke login. ADAptify — a site-wide accessibility widget (contrast, text size, grayscale, invert, readable font, highlight links, big cursor, pause motion) with its own CMS tab. Enable/disable, placement, and brand colors are stored server-side in data/adaptify.json and apply to every page instantly. One-click install drops the widget script across all pages. Plus the 1.3.0 work: server-level page list, automatic server connection, server-side settings, Revisions ledger, faithful HTML import, WAF-proof multipart save, square media picker + 16:9 desktop editor, FAQ/accordion editing, and Privacy Policy generator.
apiNote: admin/api.php holds this site's secrets (API token) and is never auto-updated. If a release changes api.php, update it by hand. 1.4.2 adds HTTPS enforcement to api.php (the credential-action backstop) — deploy the updated api.php and the new root .htaccess by hand to get the full effect; the redirect and guard only protect a site once those two files are in place. 1.4.0 adds 'adaptify.js' to api.php's repo-fetch allow-list — only needed for installing the widget from a PRIVATE repo.
